Simple modification in lifestyle like deliberately climbing up the stairs instead of using the lift and walking for short distance instead of using a vehicle could also immensely help in increasing our physical activity. Exercise program should include 'warm up' and 'cool down' periods each lasting for 5 minutes. During exercise, the intensity of exercise should ensure 60-70% increase in heart rate. Inactive men over the age of 40 years, women over the age of 50 years and people at high risk for chronic diseases like heart disease and diabetes should first consult a physician before engaging in a program of vigorous physical activity such as running and swimming.
